reasoning: The NIST 800-171 control 3.6.1 focuses on establishing an incident-handling capability, which includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring comprehensive audit logging (for detection and analysis), enforcing network policies to contain incidents, and using admission controllers to prevent misconfigurations. Audit logs provide a trail for incident detection and analysis, while network policies can help contain potential breaches. Admission controllers can enforce security policies to prevent incidents.
title: 3_6_1: 3.6.1 Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities
